name: OudsFilterChip
desc:
A filter chip is a compact UI element used in a design system to represent a filter option that can be selected or deselected by the user.
Filter chips allow users to refine content or data by applying one or more filters in a visually accessible and interactive way.
Purpose: Allows users to filter content by selecting or deselecting specific categories or attributes.
Behavior: Can be toggled on/off to refine displayed results. Selected chips remain visually distinct to indicate active filters.

name: OudsSugesstionChip
desc:
A suggestion chip is a compact UI element used to present recommended or predictive options based on user input or context.
Often found in search bars, forms, or messaging interfaces, suggestion chips help users quickly select from relevant suggestions.
They are typically non-selected by default and can be tapped or clicked to apply the suggestion, streamlining user input and enhancing usability.

name: OudsTag
desc:
A tag is a small element that shows short info like a label, keyword, or category.
It helps users quickly find, group, or understand content.
Tags can stay in place or be removed, and can include icons or actions like close or edit.

name: createState
desc: Creates the mutable state for this widget at a given location in the tree.

name: OudsTagInput
desc:
A Tag Input is a component that allows users to enter multiple values, each represented as a tag input.
As users type and submit values (usually by pressing enter, comma, or tab), each value is transformed into a Tag.
Tag Inputs are often used for adding labels, categories, or participants.
They typically support editing, removing, and validating individual tags.

desc: Creates an instance of OudsTextField, which is an alias for OudsTextInput.

name: OudsTextInput
desc:
OudsTextInput is a customizable text input field that allows users
to enter, edit, or read text.

name: createState
desc: Creates the mutable state for this widget at a given location in the tree.

name: OudsTextInputStyle
desc: The OudsTextInputStyle defines the style visual behavior and feedback.

name: values
desc: A constant List of the values in this enum, in order of their declaration.

name: OudsTextInputBackgroundColorModifier
desc: Used to apply the right background color associated to the state

name: OudsTextInputBorderModifier
desc: A class that provides the border color for the OudsTextInput based on its state and selection

name: getBorder
desc: Gets the borderSide based on the text input state and whether it is selected

name: getBorderRadius
desc:
Static method to get the border radius for a text input based on the border parameter.
Returns a BorderRadius object with the appropriate radius value.

name: getBorderSideByState
desc: Returns a BorderSide based on the given OudsTextInputControlState.

name: OudsTextInputForegroundColorModifier
desc: Used to apply the right foreground color associated to the state

name: OudsTextInputTextColorModifier
desc: A class that provides the tick color for the OudsTextInput based on its state and error status.

name: getCursorTextColor
desc: Gets the cursor text color based on the control state status.

name: getHelperTextColor
desc: Gets the helper text color based on the control state status.

name: getHintTextColor
desc: Gets the hint text color (placeholder) based on the control state status.

name: getSuffixPrefixTextColor
desc: Gets the suffix/prefix text color based on the control state status.

name: getTextColor
desc: Gets the text color based on the control state and error status.
